novel_features.name: What's new since timestamp
novel_features.description: List ads added since a timestamp for any stored named-search, with cron-friendly exit codes when matches are found.
novel_features.rationale: Pure local SQLite query over synced `ads.timestamp` — Blocket's public search API has no `since` parameter and no delta endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when you need to know what is genuinely new versus what the user already saw, without rerunning a paginated search.

novel_features.name: Arbitrage / underpriced detector
novel_features.description: Find current listings priced below threshold × median for their (make, model, year-band, mileage-band) cohort.
novel_features.rationale: Median computation requires aggregation across the synced corpus; the live API never returns price distributions.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to surface deals an agent could not detect from a single search — the comparison set lives only in the local store.

novel_features.name: Price history per ad
novel_features.description: Show every snapshotted price for an ad over time with deltas; sync populates `ad_price_snapshots` on each run.
novel_features.rationale: The Blocket ad-detail endpoint returns only the current price; without local snapshots there is no history.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to confirm a price is genuinely lower than recent asking prices, not just lower than the user remembered.

novel_features.name: Dealer portfolio with stats
novel_features.description: Aggregate every current ad for an `org_id` plus inventory count, median price, oldest-listing-days, and percent price-dropped-this-week.
novel_features.rationale: Per-dealer aggregates require joining `ads` and `ad_price_snapshots` locally; no wrapper exposes these stats.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to characterise a dealer's portfolio and turnover before contacting them, instead of scrolling their profile.

novel_features.name: Stale listings detection
novel_features.description: Listings older than N days that are still active in the most recent sync, optionally filtered by vertical or dealer.
novel_features.rationale: Blocket has no "days on market" field; the answer is computed from `ads.timestamp` versus current time joined with last-sync presence.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to find listings ripe for negotiation or to rule out stale corpora when comparing prices.

novel_features.name: Cross-vertical search
novel_features.description: Free-text search that fans across BAP, used cars, boats, motorcycles, and the ten niche mobility verticals locally and merges results.
novel_features.rationale: Every existing wrapper is per-vertical; cross-vertical FTS requires a UNION over the typed local tables.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an item could live in any vertical (e.g., a Volvo could be a car, a part in BAP, or a model in MC).

novel_features.name: Watch with price-drop notification
novel_features.description: Run a stored named-search and exit non-zero with structured JSON when ads in the result set dropped in price since the previous run.
novel_features.rationale: Diffing snapshots between runs is a local-store operation; the Blocket API will not tell you that a specific ad got cheaper.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this in cron jobs or agent scheduled tasks to react only to genuine price drops, not every refresh.

novel_features.name: Description regex
novel_features.description: Match a Go regular expression against ad description text backfilled by ad-detail sync.
novel_features.rationale: Blocket's search API matches `q` against headings and a few high-gravity fields, not the long description; description text only enters the corpus via ad-detail backfill.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when the deal-defining keyword ("rökfri", "nyservad", "original") only appears in the description body, not the title.

novel_features.name: Geo radius search
novel_features.description: Filter synced ads by point + radius using haversine distance against `coordinates`.
novel_features.rationale: Blocket's public search filters by Swedish region IDs (län), not arbitrary latitude/longitude; the per-ad coordinates only become useful in the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when the user defines proximity as "within 30 km of my postcode", not as a Swedish administrative region.

novel_features.name: Vehicle appraisal
novel_features.description: Given a make/model/year/mileage, compute p10/p50/p90 of asking prices from the synced corpus and render the distribution.
novel_features.rationale: Percentile aggregates across a comparable set live only in the local store; no API endpoint returns a market-wide price distribution.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to price a hypothetical vehicle (yours or one you are about to bid on) against the current market without scraping listings by hand.

narrative.headline: Every Blocket vertical, every filter, plus the local store, price history, arbitrage detection, and cross-vertical search no other Blocket tool offers.
narrative.value_prop: Blocket-pp-cli matches every existing wrapper's surface across the BAP and mobility verticals — including the ten niche ones (truck, bus, agriculture, caravan, ATV, scooter, mobile-home, a-tractor, agriculture-tractor, agriculture-tool) that no wrapper covers — and adds a SQLite-backed local store that unlocks workflows the live API cannot answer: what's new since yesterday, which listings are underpriced relative to their cohort, what a dealer's portfolio looks like, and how a vehicle's price compares to the rolling p10/p50/p90 distribution. Every command emits agent-native JSON with `--select` for nested fields and typed exit codes for cron and watcher pipelines.
narrative.auth_narrative: No authentication required. Every command uses Blocket's public, unauthenticated endpoints. Saved searches (Bevakningar) are intentionally out of scope in v1 — the local `watch` command is the agent-native equivalent.

quickstart.comment: Confirm the binary is healthy and the public endpoints are reachable from your network.
Most recent BAP listings matching airpods, JSON shape ready to pipe.
Used Volvos from 2010+ under 80,000 SEK — the canonical used-car query.
Sample one of the ten niche mobility verticals nobody else exposes.
Surface XC70 listings priced ≤ 80% of the local-corpus median for their year+mileage band.
Price a specific vehicle against the corpus distribution.

troubleshoots.symptom: Search returns 0 results when you expect matches
troubleshoots.fix: Run `blocket-pp-cli filters list --vertical <vertical>` to see the valid filter values; Blocket validates make/model/category enums strictly.
Add `--page-delay 1s` to slow the request rate; the public endpoints are generous but a 429 means you have run too many parallel requests for too long.
Run `blocket-pp-cli sync` first — these commands aggregate over the local store and need at least a few hundred rows in the relevant vertical.
Real estate is intentionally out of scope in v1; bostad.blocket.se rate-limits anonymous traffic aggressively. Use the website directly.

narrative.when_to_use: Use blocket-pp-cli whenever an agent task involves Swedish second-hand listings: hunting deals across BAP and mobility verticals, comparison-shopping a vehicle, characterising a dealer's portfolio, watching for price drops on stored searches, or building a structured snapshot of a corner of the Swedish market for analysis. Prefer it over generic web fetching of blocket.se — every command emits structured JSON, exposes the full filter surface, and the local store keeps history that the live site discards.

recipes.title: Five fresh BAP listings as a JSON pipeline
recipes.explanation: Narrows a verbose search response down to the agent-relevant fields; canonical for shell pipelines.

recipes.title: Used-car ranking with jq
recipes.command: blocket-pp-cli cars list --make Volvo --model XC70 --year-from 2012 --json --select docs.heading,docs.year,docs.mileage,docs.price.amount,docs.location | jq 'sort_by(.price.amount)[:10]'
recipes.explanation: Pipes structured JSON into jq for ranking — every wrapper requires a custom parser to do this.

recipes.title: Daily delta on a saved search
recipes.explanation: Cron-friendly query for a 24-hour window; exits non-zero when there are matches so a wrapper script can react.

recipes.title: Price-drop watcher
recipes.explanation: Diffs current prices against the prior snapshot; pair with a cron job or systemd timer to get notified only when the corpus actually moved.

recipes.title: Description regex against the local store
recipes.explanation: Find listings whose description (not the title) mentions "nyservad" — the public search API will not match against descriptions.

trigger_phrases: find a used car on Blocket
